concept: Workshop / Place of Making / Craft Space
description: A room or building where things are made or repaired; a space dedicated to craft. 'Shoruka' names where making happens—tools arranged, materials ready, work in progress.
sound_symbolism: The word shapes and builds. 'Sho' /ʃo/ begins with focused intention, work starting. 'Ru' rolls with ongoing effort. Then 'ka' marks with clear result, making happening.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo shoruka' (the workshop). Related to 'phaewi' (studio) but emphasizes practical craft rather than artistic creation.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Workshops enable making—spaces where communities repair, build, and create rather than just consume.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Workshops transmitted knowledge—apprentices learning beside masters, skills passing hand to hand.
